speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Charmian!

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Madam?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ha, ha! Give me to drink mandragora.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Why, madam?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That I might sleep out this great gap of time

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My Antony is away.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: You think of him too much.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, ’tis treason!

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Madam, I trust not so.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou, eunuch Mardian!

speaker: MARDIAN
text: What’s your Highness’ pleasure?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Not now to hear thee sing. I take no pleasure

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: In aught an eunuch has. ’Tis well for thee

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That, being unseminared, thy freer thoughts

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: May not fly forth of Egypt. Hast thou affections?

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Yes, gracious madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Indeed?

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Not in deed, madam, for I can do nothing

speaker: MARDIAN
text: But what indeed is honest to be done.

speaker: MARDIAN
text: Yet have I fierce affections, and think

speaker: MARDIAN
text: What Venus did with Mars.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, Charmian,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Where think’st thou he is now? Stands he, or sits he?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Or does he walk? Or is he on his horse?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O happy horse, to bear the weight of Antony!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Do bravely, horse, for wot’st thou whom thou mov’st?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The demi-Atlas of this Earth, the arm

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And burgonet of men. He’s speaking now,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Or murmuring “Where’s my serpent of old Nile?”

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: For so he calls me. Now I feed myself

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: With most delicious poison. Think on me

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That am with Phoebus’ amorous pinches black,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And wrinkled deep in time? Broad-fronted Caesar,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: When thou wast here above the ground, I was

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: A morsel for a monarch. And great Pompey

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Would stand and make his eyes grow in my brow;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: There would he anchor his aspect, and die

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: With looking on his life.

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Sovereign of Egypt, hail!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: How much unlike art thou Mark Antony!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Yet coming from him, that great med’cine hath

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: With his tinct gilded thee.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: How goes it with my brave Mark Antony?

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Last thing he did, dear queen,

speaker: ALEXAS
text: He kissed—the last of many doubled kisses—

speaker: ALEXAS
text: This orient pearl. His speech sticks in my heart.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Mine ear must pluck it thence.

speaker: ALEXAS
text: “Good friend,” quoth he,

speaker: ALEXAS
text: “Say the firm Roman to great Egypt sends

speaker: ALEXAS
text: This treasure of an oyster; at whose foot,

speaker: ALEXAS
text: To mend the petty present, I will piece

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Her opulent throne with kingdoms. All the East,

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Say thou, shall call her mistress.” So he nodded

speaker: ALEXAS
text: And soberly did mount an arm-gaunt steed,

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Who neighed so high that what I would have spoke

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Was beastly dumbed by him.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What, was he sad, or merry?

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Like to the time o’ th’ year between th’ extremes

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Of hot and cold, he was nor sad nor merry.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, well-divided disposition!—Note him,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Note him, good Charmian, ’tis the man! But note him:

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He was not sad, for he would shine on those

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That make their looks by his; he was not merry,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Which seemed to tell them his remembrance lay

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: In Egypt with his joy; but between both.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, heavenly mingle!—Be’st thou sad or merry,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The violence of either thee becomes,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: So does it no man’s else.—Met’st thou my posts?

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Ay, madam, twenty several messengers.

speaker: ALEXAS
text: Why do you send so thick?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Who’s born that day

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: When I forget to send to Antony

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Shall die a beggar.—Ink and paper, Charmian.—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Welcome, my good Alexas.—Did I, Charmian,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ever love Caesar so?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: O, that brave Caesar!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Be choked with such another emphasis!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Say “the brave Antony.”

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: The valiant Caesar!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: By Isis, I will give thee bloody teeth

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If thou with Caesar paragon again

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My man of men.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: By your most gracious pardon,

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: I sing but after you.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My salad days,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: When I was green in judgment, cold in blood,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To say as I said then. But come, away,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Get me ink and paper.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He shall have every day a several greeting,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Or I’ll unpeople Egypt.
